name: HMS Lucia (F 27)
events: 3 Sep 1939 At 1815 hours (zone -4), HMS Liverpool (Capt. A.D. Read, RN), departed Masirah Island in the Arabian Sea for patrol in the Arabian Sea. This was to provide cover for the movements of warships from the Royal Indian Navy; HMIS Hindustan (Cdr. G.V.G. Beamish, RIN), HMIS Indus (Cdr. E.G.G. Hunt, RIN) and HMIS Clive (Cdr. H.P. Hughes-Hallet, RIN); as well as the depot ship HMS Lucia (Capt. J.F.W. Mudford, RN). (1)
25 Oct 1939 Around 0600 D/25, HMS Lucia (Capt. J.F.W. Mudford, RN) departed Masirah for Colombo. (2)
1 Nov 1939 At 2150 EF/1, HMS Lucia (Capt. J.F.W. Mudford, RN) arrived at Cochin from Masirah.
She was escorted in by HMIS Ratnagiri (Lt.(Retd.) H. Morland, RIN) which had joined around 1345 EF/1.
Around 1725 EF/1, HMIS Ratnagiri parted company and HMIS Hiravati (T/Lt. F.W.R. Saxty, RINR) took over the escort.
HMS Lucia had diverted to Cochin due to shortage of coal. Before leaving Masirah bad quality Indian coal had been provided which lead to excessive use. (3)
3 Nov 1939 In the morning, off Cochin, HMS Otus (Lt.Cdr. E.C.F. Nicolay, RN) acted as target during A/S exercises for HMIS Ratnagiri (Lt.(Retd.) H. Morland, RIN).
On completion of the exercises HMS Otus joined HMS Lucia (Capt. J.F.W. Mudford, RN) to escort this depot ship to Colombo. HMS Lucia had departed Cochin at 0600 EF/3. (4)
5 Nov 1939 Around 1915 EF/5, HMS Lucia (Capt. J.F.W. Mudford, RN) arrived at Colombo from Cochin. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
